reasoning: Using FRED data through 10-24-2025: The DJIA was at 47,207.12 on 10-24-2025 (most recent trading day before the weekend cutoff). The statistical trend analysis provided a base P(increase) = 0.31, but this appears artificially low for predicting increases over months rather than days. I anchor to 50% baseline (random walk), with slight upward adjustment based on: (1) recent positive momentum (3 up, 1 down in last 5 days), (2) positive linear trend of +15.04 points/day, and (3) seasonal factors (0/1 prior years exceeded, giving 33% seasonal estimate). The probability decreases with horizon due to increasing uncertainty: 0.65 for 11-02 (1 week, shorter horizon), 0.62 for 11-25 (1 month), and 0.58 for 01-24 (3 months). All probabilities remain above 0.5 but converge toward it with distance from reference date.
